description: Maintenance of optimum temperature during transport and care

description: Universal salt iodization to prevent deficiency.
standard: Consumer level salt must contain 15 ppm iodine.

description: Iron and folic acid supplementation for high-risk children.
dosage: Folifer Paed: 20mg elemental iron + 100mcg folic acid for 3 months.

schedule: 9 doses between 9 months and 5 years, every 6 months.

method: Expose babies to sunlight after oiling.

rule: Avoid for first 2 years to prevent iron deficiency and GI blood loss.

steps: Soak grain with 3x water for 12 hours
Drain and wrap in moist muslin cloth for 48 hours to germinate
Dry germinated grain in sunlight for 8 hours
Roast at 80°C for 10 mins until brittle

rules: Use minimum water for boiling; use leftover water for soups
Avoid deep frying at high temperatures (toxic volatiles)
Steam or pressure cook for maximum nutrient retention
Cook rice with 2 to 2.5 times water

type_i.definition: Regulatory min/max (e.g., added Vitamin C, low fat)

poverty_line_baseline: Rs 372 per capita per month (2000 AD)

mechanism: Balance SIRS/CARS by modulating pro/anti-inflammatory cytokines

mandatory: Consistent weight gain (>5 g/kg/day for 3 days)

description: Achieve a match between energy intake and energy expenditure to prevent obesity.

components.intake: Calorie content of food (4 kcal/g carb/protein, 9 kcal/g fat)

description: Increase Non-Exercise Activity Thermogenesis (NEAT) to enhance daily energy expenditure.
Assess risk of metabolic disease using WHR.
Prevent early onset of adult diseases by ensuring adequate intrauterine nutrition and avoiding rapid postnatal overfeeding.
focus: Balanced catch-up growth without excessive fat deposition.

description: Weight loss through structured calorie reduction.
diet_types: Very Low Calorie Diet (VLCD): < 800 kcal/day (max 4 weeks)

moderate_intensity_test: Breathing harder but can still talk comfortably.
avoidance: Sit for no more than 30 minutes at a time.

description: Pancreatic lipase inhibitor for obesity management.

strategy: Directly observed swallow pill on Saturdays
rationale: Intestinal mucosal cells renew every 4-5 days; weekly doses are theoretically conclusive for prophylaxis.

target: Clinically deficient or severe PEM children
regimen: 1 lakh units (< 1 yr) or 2 lakh units (> 1 yr) x 2 doses on consecutive days; repeat at 6 months.
special_note: measles cases must receive one dose.

strategies: 3 Gs: Greens, Grams, and Grains (Iron rich)
Avoid coffee/tea with meals (hinders iron absorption)
Include lime juice/citrus (Vitamin C enhances iron/calcium absorption)
GYOR: Green, Yellow, Orange, Red vegetables (Rainbow Revolution)

standard: 30-50 ppm potassium iodate at manufacturing; 15 ppm at consumer level.

description: Standard calculation for maintenance calories and fluid based on weight.
Replacement of extra losses based on percentage body weight deficit.
strategy: Correct 2/3 of calculated deficit (approx 20-100 ml/kg) in 3-6 hours.

description: Calorie and protein requirements during acute illness and stress.
hospital_calculation: 150-200 kcal/kg (observed weight) in moderate to severe malnutrition.
fat_guideline: 25-45% of total calories; include MCT for malabsorption.

maintenance: 1 ml of 15% KCl added to 100 ml IV fluid (2 mEq/L)
hypokalaemia_correction: Up to 2 ml of 15% KCl per 100 ml IV fluid (40 mEq/L)
precaution: Do not administer if IV fluid is not continued unless hypokalaemia is documented.

principle: If the gut works, use it.

description: Parenteral nutrition for patients who cannot eat, should not eat, or will not eat.

weaning_protocol: Stop TPN only when 70% of kilocalories are taken orally/enterally.

description: WHO/UNICEF strategy for treating dehydration in diarrhoeal diseases.

plans.plan_a: No dehydration: 1/2 to 1 glass/purge of ORS or Home Available Fluids (HAF).
plans.plan_b: Some dehydration: 70-100 ml/kg ORS over 4 hours.
plans.plan_c: Severe dehydration: 100 ml/kg IV Ringer Lactate/Normal Saline over 3-6 hours.

description: Standard 12% Partial Parenteral Nutrition solution preparation.
Formulas to estimate caloric needs of the critically ill child.
Dietary escalation for persistent or chronic diarrhoea.

levels.level_2_milk_free: Cereal-pulse mix (SAT Mix) or amylase rich food (ARF).
levels.level_3_lactose_sucrose_free: Chicken glucose puree or soya-based formulas.

precaution: Avoid exercise if RBS > 300 mg/dl; No extra CHO if RBS 180-300 mg/dl.

description: High-carbohydrate, high-fibre, low-fat diet for cardiovascular health.

description: Management of Phenylketonuria.

description: Acceptable plasma levels for MSUD management.

action: Ensure strict hygiene across all 6 channels to prevent bacterial transmission.

description: Daily energy and micronutrient requirements for the transitional growth stage.

description: Breaking the intergenerational cycle of growth failure.

principles: Moderation: Avoid 'empty calories' from soft drinks and snacks.

description: Extra nutritional requirements during pregnancy and lactation.

pregnancy.slogan: Eating one extra meal for the baby.

rubella: 1 dose for girls at 12-13 years (if not given earlier).
hepatitis_b: 3 doses (0, 1, 6 months) if not given earlier.

description: Nutrition principles to delay aging effects and manage age-related physiological changes.

formula_options: 1 ml water per 1 kcal consumed (e.g., 1.8 L for 1800 kcal)
25-30 ml water per 1 kg body weight

description: Management of helminthic reservoir to break the transmission chain.

description: Daily energy targets for strength and endurance athletes.

carbohydrates.logic: Maintain muscle glycogen stores to prevent power drop.

description: Systematic fluid replacement to ensure safety and performance.
pre_exercise: 15-20 oz (2-3 hours before) + 8-10 oz (15 mins before).
post_exercise: 16-24 oz (2-3 glasses) for every 1 pound weight loss.

description: Refueling logic for glycogen replenishment and muscle repair.
fuel_priority: Glucose (Energy bars/drinks) for immediate muscle glucose restoration.

description: Maximum daily intake to regulate blood pressure and volume.
caution: Hidden sodium in monosodium glutamate, sodium nitrite, and processed meats.

description: Approximate calculation for daily kcal and ml requirements.
note: Energy (kcal) and Fluid (ml) values are approximately equivalent numerically in this simplified scale.

description: Strategic nutrient timing to maximize glycogen and minimize GI distress.

timeline.3_4_hours_before: Solid meal: Pasta, Baked potato, Cereal with low-fat milk, Toast with lean meat.
timeline.2_3_hours_before: Snack: Bread, Bagels, Low-fat yogurt, Fresh fruit.
timeline.1_hour_before: Liquid/Light: Fruit juice, Energy gels, 1.5 cups sports drink.

description: Metabolic shift logic for performance optimization.
lactate_threshold: Point where lactic acid build-up causes fatigue; requires intensity reduction.
efficiency_gain: Aerobic metabolism is 20x more efficient at generating ATP from carbohydrates than anaerobic glycolysis.

description: Strategic management of growth correction in previously malnourished or SGA infants.
objectives: Avoid rapid infancy surges to prevent subsequent obesity.
Monitor weight-for-length and adiposity alongside weight gain.
Maintain a steady, non-aggressive trajectory to avoid early insulin resistance.
logic: Attempts to augment growth too rapidly often result in undue fatness and metabolic syndrome rather than healthy height.

description: Standard progress markers across four key domains.

fine_motor_adaptive.1_year: Tower of 2 cubes; releases on request

language.2_years: Joins 2 words; refers to self as 'I'

personal_social.6_months: Mirror play; stranger anxiety; drinks from cup
personal_social.3_years: Dresses and undresses (with help); Gender identity

toilet_training.10_months: Can be made to sit on toilet seat
toilet_training.18_months: Walks to toilet; Dry by day

description: Quantitative measure of developmental progress.
interpretation: Evaluates maturity and behaviour across functional areas. Dissociation between areas (e.g., advanced in motor but retarded in language) requires separate evaluation.

description: Critical targets for the first 24 months of life.
Framework for assessing mental capability and stages of development.
Essential health care services universally accessible (Alma Ata).

description: Specific targets for restoring malnourished children (PEM).
rehabilitation_strategy: To get well and keep well via centre or home-based care.

description: Classification of family status based on updated Indian standards.

logic: Values defined to pass through BMI of 25 (Overweight) and 30 (Obese) at Age 18.

description: Adherence to the IMS Act (1992/1994) to protect breastfeeding.
prohibitions: No advertisement of infant milk substitutes or feeding bottles
